project_overview.project_name: Horn of Africa Initiative: Djibouti Regional Economic Corridor
project_overview.project_development_objective: To improve regional connectivity and enhance logistics efficiency in Djibouti along the Djibouti-Addis southern corridor
project_overview.total_financing: US$90 million (US$70 million IDA, US$17 million GoD, US$3 million private sector)
project_overview.beneficiaries: Local consumers and producers of tradable goods in Djibouti and hinterland, road transport service providers, local traders, populations living in corridor area, end consumers, port authorities

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: yes - Djibouti exited FCV list in FY21 but remains vulnerable to regional spillovers from Horn of Africa conflicts and instability

key_components.component_name: Improvement of infrastructure and introduction of intelligent transportation systems along the Djibouti-Addis southern corridor
key_components.description: Rehabilitation of 42km of roads (Djibouti-Arta 21km, RN5 junction-Ali Sabbieh 12km, Ali Sabbieh-Guelileh 9km) with climate resilient standards, deployment of Integrated Transport Information System (ITIS), maintenance and operation through OPBRC

key_components.component_name: Improvement of transit services along the Djibouti-Addis southern corridor
key_components.description: Enhancement of customs transit procedures, development of OSBP at Guelileh border, upgrade of Djibouti customs automated systems including Cargo Targeting System

key_components.component_name: Institutional strengthening and capacity-building on road performance-based contracts and project implementation support
key_components.description: Technical assistance for OPBRC, institutional strengthening on road PBCs and PPPs, maximizing corridor development impacts for local communities including HIV/AIDS prevention and women's economic empowerment
Emergency response component to be activated during eligible crisis or emergency

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: ADR (Djibouti Road Agency) under Ministry of Infrastructure and Equipment (MoIE)
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Inter-ministerial Project Steering Committee led by MoIE, Project Coordination Unit (PCU) within ADR, Supporting Implementing Agencies including DPCR and Customs
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: Open international competition for major contracts, OPBRC modality for road works, WB Standard Procurement Documents
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Quarterly implementation progress reports, annual monitoring and evaluation reports, satisfaction surveys, PCU responsible for M&E with inputs from beneficiary agencies

identified_risks.risk_description: Ability to influence private sector finance mobilization depends on political will; line ministries may lack incentives or resist change
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Obtain commitment at highest state level, involve donor agencies and stakeholders to emphasize project importance

identified_risks.risk_description: Regional fragility in Horn of Africa, political instability and conflict in sub-region including recent developments in Ethiopia, vulnerability to spillover and refugee influxes
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Regular security risk assessments, activities requiring Ethiopia collaboration conditional on mutual understanding between customs authorities

identified_risks.risk_description: Impact of COVID-19 pandemic on GDP growth and unemployment, job losses from decreased goods volume through Djibouti ports affecting tax base and government contribution to road maintenance
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Monitor economic conditions and adjust implementation as needed

identified_risks.risk_description: ADR has limited experience with World Bank financed projects, multi-sectoral operation complexity, need for harmonization with Ethiopia
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Capacity building, hiring experienced specialists, technical assistance, consultant support

identified_risks.risk_description: No current WB transport project in country, ADR lacks WB project experience, limited procurement capacity for complex OPBRC contracts
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Hire experienced FM and procurement specialists, provide training, consultant support for OPBRC procurement and supervision

identified_risks.risk_description: Potential erosion, vegetation loss, construction pollution, occupational and community health safety, road safety risks, livelihood impacts, COVID-19 exposure, SEA/SH risks
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: ESIA with ESMP, Labor Management Procedures, Resettlement Framework, Road Safety Management Plan, Stakeholder Engagement Plan, Environmental and Social Commitment Plan

identified_risks.risk_description: Multiple stakeholder participation required, coordination risk between Djibouti and Ethiopia, need for integrated effort on regional integration and trade facilitation
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Close collaboration between SOP project teams, leverage HoAI dialogue platform, facilitate Djibouti-Ethiopia dialogue

triggered_safeguard_policies: ESS1: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts
ESS2: Stakeholder Engagement and Information Disclosure
ESS4: Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention
ESS6: Land Acquisition and Involuntary Resettlement
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - project-level grievance redress mechanisms and World Bank corporate Grievance Redress Service available
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Activities to address gender gaps including literacy training for women, support for female labor force participation, childcare services at border areas, GBV prevention protocols, 20% quota for women in construction jobs

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project designed to support regional integration and cooperation through Horn of Africa Initiative, addresses regional fragility through economic connectivity, includes coordination mechanisms between countries
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Djibouti recently exited FCV list but remains vulnerable to regional spillovers from Horn of Africa conflicts, weak institutional capacity, governance challenges, vulnerability to refugees and migrants from regional crises
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Security situation monitored through periodic threat assessments, activities requiring Ethiopia collaboration conditional on customs authorities mutual understanding, project implementation may be affected by regional security context
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - Djibouti serves as major destination for refugees and migrants fleeing political and environmental crises in Horn of Africa region, typically settling in poor areas worsening conditions
